events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves missing nip-point guards on a conveyor system, which is a serious safety hazard. Nip points are pinch points where moving parts can catch clothing, fingers, or body parts. Missing guards is a direct OSHA violation.

Key points:
- Missing nip-point guards = HAZ (hazard)
- OSHA 1910.212 requires guarding of points of operation and nip points
- CEMA also requires guarding for conveyor nip points
- Work is in-progress, so the conveyor may be running
- This is a critical safety issue - someone could be seriously injured

Decision: fail (dangerous/non-compliant)
Risk: critical (missing guards on running conveyor = imminent danger)
Component conditions: installed-defective (guards missing/defective), non-compliant (violates OSHA/CEMA)
Percent complete: I'll estimate based on the work being in-progress, maybe 50%
Confidence: high, 0.95
Findings: missing nip-point guards
Actions: stop work, lockout/tagout, install guards before resuming
References: OSHA 1910.212, CEMA

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing nip-point guards on conveyor system exposing operators to pinch/crush hazards at rotating drums, rollers, and belt interfaces",
    "Conveyor operating in-progress without required point-of-operation guarding — direct OSHA 1910.212 violation",
    "CEMA-compliant nip-point guarding not installed or has been removed, leaving rotating components accessible"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop the conveyor and initiate lockout/tagout (LOTO) to de-energize the system",
    "Restrict access to the unguarded conveyor section and barricade the hazard zone",
    "Install or reinstall CEMA/OSHA-compliant nip-point guards at all pinch points (head pulley, tail pulley, snub rollers, idler junctions, transfer points) before resuming operation",
    "Inspect entire conveyor run for additional missing or damaged guards and verify all guarding is secured before re-energizing",
    "Document the deficiency, notify production supervision, and do not resume standard work until guarding passes inspection"
  ],
  "rationale": "Missing nip-point guards on an actively running conveyor create an imminent risk of entanglement, crushing, and amputation injuries. OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) and (a)(2) require that points of operation and nip points be guarded to prevent employee contact with moving parts. CEMA standards similarly mandate guarding at all accessible nip points. The condition is both installed-defective (guarding absent or removed) and non-compliant, requiring immediate shutdown and correction before work continues.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) — Types of guarding: one or more methods of machine guarding shall be provided to protect the operator and other employees from hazards such as nip points",
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(2) — General requirements for machine guards: guards shall be affixed and secured",
    "CEMA — Conveyor Equipment Manufacturers Association standards for nip-point and pinch-point guarding on conveyor systems"
  ]
}
